item.title: Largest LEGO Set Ever Made Reportedly Coming July 1
item.description:
This article was updated by Andrea Trama on March 18, 2026, with reference to the largest LEGO sets ever to include a recent Pokemon kit. It was first published on March 17.

The biggest LEGO set ever based on Barcelona's Sagrada Família basilica is reportedly launching in summer 2026, according to some new insights from a reliable leaker. The upcoming model is expected to include hundreds more pieces than the current largest LEGO set in existence.

The new information comes from the Brick Tap community's Chief Wiggum, a leaker with a long track record of credible LEGO product insights. The same source just recently shared the purported details of LEGO's first Pokemon set with minifigures.

21065 Sagrada Família Reportedly Poised to Seize the Title of Largest LEGO Set Ever

The insider's latest report materialized on March 17, identifying a new set in the form of 21065 Sagrada Família. At 12,060 pieces, the unannounced model is expected to debut as the Danish toymaker's largest set ever by some margin. 21065 Sagrada Família is reportedly planned to hit the store shelves as part of LEGO's early summer 2026 product wave, with Chief Wiggum citing July 1 as its official release date. It is expected to retail for $599.99 in the United States, as per the same source.

Who’s That Character?

Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.

5.0 s
0 / 10

Results

0

High Score: 0

—

More Games
Who’s That Character? Identify the silhouettes before time runs out.
Easy (7.5s)Medium (5.0s)Hard (2.5s)Permadeath (2.5s)
5.0 s
0 / 10
Results
0
High Score: 0 —
More Games

21065 Sagrada Família is said to be part of the LEGO Architecture theme, according to both the newly emerged leak and separate Brick Tap reporting from January 2026. That stated categorization is notable because the Architecture line has so far largely been known for more compact, display-focused landmark and skyline models rather than replicas competing with LEGO's biggest collector releases. Nonetheless, a Sagrada Família build on this scale would fit the line's emphasis on famous real-world structures, even if its reported dimensions suggest something much closer to the company’s ultra-premium showcase sets than a typical Architecture-line release.

Top 10 Largest LEGO Sets Ever as of March 2026

#

Set

Pieces

75331

Star Wars

10

LOTR: Rivendell

6,167

10316

Icons

Mar 5, 2023

N/A

$499.99

18+

Order the Largest-Ever LEGO Star Wars Set Today!

The Timing of LEGO's Rumored Sagrada Família Model Is Not Accidental

The real-life landmark on which the rumored LEGO set is based is a Roman Catholic basilica in Barcelona and the best-known work of Catalan architect Antoni Gaudí. Located in the city's grid-based Eixample district, Sagrada Família combines elements of Gothic Revival and Catalan Modernism. Construction began in 1882, and Gaudí took over the project the following year, transforming it into a highly symbolic, nature-inspired design defined by soaring towers, sculptural facade, and intricate geometric forms.

Subscribe to the newsletter for deeper LEGO set insights

Curious how the rumored Sagrada Família fits into LEGO's biggest sets? Subscribe to our newsletter for in-depth coverage, expert analysis, detailed comparisons and confirmed specs on major LEGO releases and collector models.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

More than 140 years later, the church remains unfinished but active. The basilica says construction is still ongoing and has projected completion of the main structure in 2026, while recent reports indicate that the central Tower of Jesus Christ has reached its full external height, with interior and finishing work still remaining. LEGO's rumored set could thus be based on a relatively finished model of the basilica, commemorating the end of nearly 150 years' worth of construction.

Another Huge LEGO Set Reportedly Coming Before 21065 Sagrada Família

21065 Sagrada Família is expected to surpass the current largest LEGO set, 31203 World Map, by 365 pieces. It is also not the only massive model the Danish toymaker is expected to debut this year; according to several earlier reports, the still-unannounced 11377 Minas Tirith will rank among the 10 largest LEGO sets ever when it launches on June 1. While it is expected to place sixth, the rumored arrival of 21065 Sagrada Família would push it down to seventh among the company's largest brick-built models to date.

item.title: How to Get Concrete in Pokopia
item.description:
There are dozens of resources and materials in Pokopia , and each one has its own purpose. Even though they have access to the required materials before, players will only be able to make concrete once they reach the fourth area in Pokopia : Sparkling Skylands.

This guide tells players how they can make concrete in Pokopia , which is necessary in order to progress through the main storyline, construct buildings, and craft several recipes.

How to Make Concrete in Pokopia

In Pokopia , players can make concrete by putting Limestone in a Concrete Mixer and having a Pokemon with Crush work on it . Players will come across Limestone when they visit Rocky Ridges, as there's plenty of it in the cave west of the Pokemon Center. However, resources in town will not replenish, so gamers might want to farm Limestone by visiting the Clefairy Doll Dream Island.

As players make their way through Pokopia , they will also meet a few Pokemon with the Crush ability. However, they will only unlock the Concrete Mixer recipe once they get to Sparkling Skylands .

To unlock the Concrete Mixer recipe in Pokopia , players have to enter Sparkling Skylands and speak to Tinkmaster. They will then receive a quest that requires them to befriend Conkeldurr, who will give them the recipe.

Gamers can find Conkeldurr by building a Construction-site generator habitat, which can be built by placing 2 Iron Scaffolds and 1 Iron Pipe by a Furnace.

Players need 2 Iron Ingots in order to make a Concrete Mixer on the crafting table. Once it's been crafted, players have to place the Concrete Mixer by the water for it to work.

Subscribe to the Newsletter for Pokopia Crafting Tips

Get deeper Pokopia crafting resources by subscribing to the newsletter. Focused guides and practical how-tos on in-game building, material management, and crafting strategies will help you tackle construction and recipe challenges with confidence.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Then, all they have to do is place the Limestone into the mixer and call over a Pokemon with Crush. They will start working on it, and it might take some time, so players can focus on another task. Each Limestone tile in Pokopia will turn into four bricks of concrete .

9 /10

Pokemon Pokopia

Follow
Followed
Life Simulation

item.title: Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen Trick Can Save You Serious Money on Repels
item.description:
In Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen , players can save some money on buying Repels and instead use HM01, Cut, to remove patches of tall grass and avoid wild Pokemon battles in the process. However, Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen players must make some progress in the main story before they can use Cut outside of battles.

Throughout Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen , moves can be taught to Pokemon in a few ways. While Pokemon can naturally learn moves by leveling up, some of the more powerful battle moves, including Ice Beam and Flamethrower, can also be taught via Technical Machines (TMs) or Hidden Machines (HMs). While TMs are a one-time consumable, HMs can be used as many times as a trainer wants. However, moves taught by HMs, including Cut, Fly, and Strength, cannot be forgotten easily by a Pokemon. In Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen , HM moves can only be forgotten by speaking to the Move Deleter in Fuchsia City.

You Can Cut the Tall Grass in Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen

Along with being a necessary HM for navigating different routes, Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen players can use Cut to remove tall grass. This clever trick was pointed out by Reddit user ch1821 on the Pokemon FireRed subreddit. Although Cut is most commonly used to remove specific trees, it can also cut down patches of tall grass in small batches of four tiles. This removes wild Pokemon from appearing when exploring some of the game's routes, though the grass does respawn upon closing the game or by entering a different area. Given that tall grass causes wild Pokemon to appear, this can be handy for saving money on Repels, which normally stop Wild Pokemon from appearing for up to 100 steps. Likewise, Super Repels and Max Repels prevent wild encounters for 200 and 250 steps, respectively. Though Repels cost 350 Poke Dollars, fledgling Pokemon trainers must use their money sparingly. However, once Cut is acquired, the money saved can instead be spent on held items in Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen , or on evergreen consumables, such as Potions and Poke Balls.

Balance the critic averages

Critical Mass

Pick games to balance the averages.

Time: 00:00
Diff: —

Scales

Multiplier ×1.00
Left
Avg —
Right
Avg —

Results

0
—
More Games

Choose a game

Balance the critic averages
Easy (6)Medium (8)Hard (10)

Critical Mass

Pick games to balance the averages.
Time: 00:00
Diff: —

Scales

Multiplier ×1.00
Left
Avg —
Right
Avg —
Results
0
—
More Games
Choose a game

However, unlocking Cut in Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen requires trainers to make some progress in the early parts of the main story. First, Pokemon trainers must defeat Misty in Cerulean City to earn the Cascade Badge. This badge allows Cut to be used outside of battle, and all Pokemon up to level 30 will obey their trainer. Additionally, HM01 (Cut) must be obtained aboard the S.S. Anne, which is harbored in Vermilion City. After acquiring an S.S. Ticket from Bill, trainers can board the ship and find the captain. After relieving the captain of his illness, he offers HM01 as a reward. Cut can then be used to open the path to the Vermilion City Gym and allow for tall grass to be removed temporarily.

Cutting Down Tall Grass Did Not Make It Past Pokemon's Third Generation

In a Pokemon battle, Cut is a Normal-type attack with 50 base power. Despite its weak attack power compared to other moves like Slash and Body Slam, Cut can carve down a wild Pokemon's HP little by little, making it easier to catch without knocking it out. Cut can be used by various Pokemon found throughout FireRed and LeafGreen , including Grass-type Pokemon like Venusaur, Victreebel, and Vileplume. However, Cut's ability to remove tall grass was removed in Gen 4, starting with Pokemon Diamond and Pearl . Ergo, Pokemon games released during Generations 1 through 3 were the only ones that could use Cut in this way.

Fortunately, tricks like the Nugget Bridge glitch and Cut's ability to mow down tall grass still work in the Switch ports of Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen . With Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen now available for more trainers to experience, time will tell what secrets players will rediscover for themselves.

Pokemon FireRed and LeafGreen

Follow
Followed

Adventure

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Set off on a grand adventure to fulfill your dreams of becoming a Pokémon Master!

Explore the Kanto region and discover wild Pokémon around every corner. Build your Pokémon collection and train, and battle your way to success–earn your badges as you develop winning strategies to use against experienced Gym Leaders in every town. Explore every inch to uncover amazing secrets that will help you in your quest to be the best trainer ever!

• Trade, battle, and chat wirelessly! All new wireless adapter comes packed in every game, so trainers can trade, battle, and chat between their FireRed and LeafGreen versions with no cables!

• Catch loads of Pokémon in the never-before-seen island areas!

• Expand your collection when you trade with a friend. Link up with Pokémon Ruby & Sapphire or Pokémon Colosseum to catch them all!

Powered by

item.title: Overwatch Introduces Map Changes After Conquest Event, But Fans Are Having Buyer's Remorse
item.description:
Overwatch has introduced some changes to the Numbani map to celebrate Overwatch’s victory in the Conquest event, prompting players to wonder what would have happened if Talon won instead. Now that the Conquest event has wrapped up, this cosmetic map update is live in Overwatch for all fans to see.

Reign of Talon Season 1: Conquest introduced some big changes to Overwatch when it launched on February 10. Among them, one of the most engaging was the eponymous Conquest event. Each week for five weeks, players could fight for Overwatch or Talon, complete challenges to earn rewards, and ultimately claim victory for one side over the other. Both teams were tied going into the final week, with Overwatch barely claiming the win by the end of the event on March 16.

Numbani Map Changes Celebrate Overwatch’s Conquest Victory

Now that Conquest has ended, Overwatch is celebrating the heroes' victory with some actual changes to one of its maps. As shared by Overwatch Cavalry on Twitter, Numbani is awash with celebration of Overwatch’s triumph over Talon. Fireworks explode above the skyline, confetti rains down on the streets, and banners celebrating Overwatch and Numbani hero Efi can be seen all over the city.

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

Other map updates from Reign of Talon Season 1, including the destruction of Watchpoint: Gibraltar and Vendetta’s takeover of Rialto and Colosseo, are still live.

At press time, it is unclear exactly how long these cosmetic changes will remain on Numbani. At the very least, they will likely stay until Season 2 begins on April 14, if not longer. Either way, while Vendetta’s offensive has been repelled for now, Talon’s new leader in Overwatch is not giving up just yet.

It is exciting that Overwatch community choices in completing Conquest Prestige Passes actually affected one of its maps. It also shows how Blizzard is pulling out all the stops with the hero shooter’s massive relaunch. Season 1 has set a high bar for Overwatch , so fans will have to see what other surprises are in store during the rest of the Reign of Talon seasons this year.

What Would Have Happened If Talon Won the Overwatch Conquest Event?

Notably, the changes to the Numbani map went live almost immediately after Overwatch was locked in as the victor of the Conquest event. This means the developers had prepared these cosmetic updates ahead of time, which also implies there was a version ready if Talon was victorious instead. This begs the important question of which map would have changed if Talon had won, and how it would have been updated.

The changes would likely have been purely cosmetic, rather than actual battlefield-altering destruction as was unleashed on Gibraltar. Perhaps aircraft, cannons, and propaganda posters would have been hung on Numbani or another map to signify Vendetta’s hostile takeover of Overwatch .

It is hard to say exactly what would have happened, but Blizzard probably has a Talon-controlled map variant tucked away just in case. Unfortunately, since Overwatch was victorious, it’s unlikely that it will ever be seen, unless Blizzard runs some kind of “what if” event to show off this and other unused map variants from theoretical future community events.

While players enjoyed the Conquest event as a whole, the result has led to some buyer’s remorse. While Overwatch winning makes a lot of sense, many fans are now wondering if it would have been more interesting if Talon had won – especially if Blizzard actually reflects the results of the Conquest event in the story. It is currently unclear if the results of this event are canonical or cosmetic. Players may never know for certain, but one thing is for sure: Overwatch is pulling out all the stops this year with Reign of Talon.

6 /10

Overwatch (Formerly Overwatch 2)

Follow
Followed
Shooter
FPS
MOBA
Multiplayer
Systems
OpenCritic Reviews

Developer(s)

Publisher(s)
Blizzard
8 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

SUBSCRIPTION
DIGITAL

OVERWATCH®, A FUTURE WORTH FIGHTING FOR! Overwatch® is an always-on and ever-evolving free-to-play, team-based action game set in an optimistic future.

Overwatch® is an always-on and ever-evolving free-to-play, team-based hero shooter game set in an optimistic future, where every match is the ultimate 5v5 or 6v6 battlefield brawl featuring new heroes and maps, different ways to play, and unique cosmetics! Lead the charge, ambush your enemies, or aid your allies as one of Overwatch’s 40+ distinct heroes. Team up with friends, take them into battle across 25+ futuristic maps inspired by real-world locations, and master multiple unique game modes.

Reign of Talon - Season 1: Conquest
The Next Big Story Begins

The world shifts as 2026 begins a year-long narrative arc where Overwatch and Talon face off in the fight for the future, joined by five new Heroes at once. Step into the story and shape what comes next!

After ten years, Season 1 opens the first chapter of Overwatch's next big story, where your choices will put you right into the fight for the future!

For the first time, five new Heroes hit the roster together, bringing new playstyles, new dynamics, and new voices into the fray all at once. Jet Pack Cat, Anran, Domina, Emre, and Mizuki arrive just in time for Reign of Talon: Conquest, launching a year-long narrative where conflict takes center stage and you contribute to how it unfolds.

Choose where you stand as Overwatch and Talon collide in a faction-driven event with outcomes that will leave visible marks on the world and your legacy. Along the way, Competitive gets a refresh and Core gameplay evolves, changing how things feel across the board.

This season sets a fresh tone for how the Overwatch story is told and how you play it.

Multiplayer
Online Multiplayer, Online Co-Op

item.title: 5 Open-World Games That Do Exploration Better Than Anyone Else
item.description:
Exploration is a word that gets thrown around a lot in the world of games. Open-world titles are notorious for advertising it, because why make an open-world game if exploration is not a major part of it? Arguably, some of the best open-world games that excel at satisfying exploration include games like Minecraft, The Elder Scrolls 5: Skyrim , and Subnautica . It's not just the thrill of knowing you can go anywhere you like, but also the reward that you get for going deeper and deeper into unknown territory. That sense of wonder isn't easy to nail.

That's why we're only highlighting the best of the best when it comes to open-world games and exploration in this list. Every title here must deliver interesting worlds, great environmental storytelling (or, alternatively, good terrain generation), and impeccable atmosphere that keeps you immersed for hours on end. There are a hundred games out there that aspire to be exactly that, but these five are arguably the top dogs of exploration.

The Elder Scrolls 5: Skyrim

An Unmatched Sense of Wonder With Breathtaking Visuals

Bethesda's Elder Scrolls games have always been favorites when discussing great exploration, and I'll admit, it was a tough choice between Skyrim and Morrowind . Ultimately, Skyrim wins thanks to simply being the newer and more accessible game, but not to worry, Morrowind fans, we'll discuss the third Elder Scrolls game briefly below. As for Skyrim , there's a lot this fifth game gets right with its world and exploration. After Helgen, you're free to go anywhere you like, the whole world opening up to you. It's possible to stumble upon some huge and tough dungeons early on in the game if you opt not to run after the main quest, and this is good because the game's best content is hidden in its side quests.

The Daedric quests in particular are fantastic, full of surprises, powerful weapons, and quirky characters. But beyond just side quest hunting, Skyrim's vistas are gorgeous, and with different biomes to find in each hold, from the autumnal forests of the Rift to the misty swamps of Hjaalmarch, you won't get bored traveling on foot. In true Bethesda style, the world is full of POIs to discover with loot, secrets, and enemies. You'll always stumble upon something unexpected that tells a story, whether it's a shrine to a Daedric god, a necromancer in the middle of resurrecting his friends, or, Talos forbid, a Dragon Priest wandering the ruins of his temple.

Honorable Mention: Morrowind

It wouldn't be fair to highlight some of the ways Morrowind nailed exploration all those years ago, and still does. Out of all the Elder Scrolls games, it has by far the most exotic, alien-feeling setting, where you truly feel like you're in a different place that's new and unknown to you. That sense of awe and mystery is so powerful in an open-world game, but tough to execute. Plus, it has a really quick start compared to Oblivion and Skyrim , for instance. In just a few blips, you can be out of the intro section and freely exploring. Who doesn't love that?

Subnautica

No One Does Underwater Exploration Better

An open-world survival crafting game, Subnautica is a story about how your ship crashlands on an ocean planet, and you're on your own to figure out how to escape. Of course, as with all open-world games, the end goal isn't really the most important thing at all. In fact, it's all about the journey and the sense of wonder here, as you begin to explore the lush, rich, and alien depths of the planet. It's a game that did a particularly fine job of this, too, by allowing you to explore further and further beneath the waves as you expand your arsenal of tools and gear, and the more you discover new resources. Eventually, you can even build your own underwater base.

The design of the creatures, from the small, cute ones that are harmless to terrifying Reapers, absolutely hits the nail on the head, and that's all without getting into the myriad of underwater biomes you'll uncover during your dives. One thing to note, because the game is deceptively light-hearted looking on Steam (despite having a horror tag!): if you do suffer from thalassophobia, please proceed with caution. The underwater gameplay is so well done and real that it can actually trigger a pretty real panic in anyone who feels uncomfortable with depths. An achievement of its own!

Elden Ring

FromSoftware's Worldbuilding Is All Show, Don't Tell

A great world doesn't have to explain its lore, and Elden Ring is living proof of that. FromSoftware was never exactly known for being candid with story and lore anyway, but the open-world environment of Elden Ring only further solidified that. The Lands Between is a massive and gorgeous world filled to the brim with dungeons, caves, wandering enemies of all sorts of kinds, secrets, and most importantly, verticality (the DLC does an even better job at this). Each area has its own atmosphere. Limgrave is the lush, green starting area, Caelid urges caution with its lake of rot and horrid enemies, and Liurnia of the Lakes is a mystical environment covered in mist with a great castle sitting in the middle.

You truly feel like an adventurer plopped in the middle of a high fantasy novel, each scene like a painting. The game also features a ton of absurd and gorgeous setpieces, such as the massive corpse on a throne inside the Dragonkin Soldier boss area in the underground areas of Elden Ring . The whole Ainsel River, Nokstella, and Siofra underground locales are awe-inspiring on their own, surreal and darkly whimsical in a way that makes you wonder what kind of mind came up with such an elaborate world. And the important thing is, exploration is never a chore, because the game's myriad of weapons, summons, ashes of war, and crucial upgrade materials are scattered all around the world. There's always something cool to discover around the next corner.

Outer Wilds

If You Want To Solve The Mystery, Keep Exploring

Exploration as part of the story of the game is when it really starts to shine and matter. It's not just a fun side feature in Outer Wilds ; it's the whole point. You're a brand-new astronaut about to launch into space and travel where no one has set foot before. What could be a more exciting prospect than that? Within the first half an hour of this game, you'll soon realize that things are a little more complicated than that, that there's a reason why you're out here: the solar system is stuck in some sort of time loop that sends you right back to the start of the game after every cycle.

Subscribe for deeper exploration guides and game insights

Get more from your next gaming adventure by subscribing to our newsletter for curated deep-dives on exploration, hidden areas, worldbuilding analysis, and pick lists that highlight games built around discovery.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Each time you head back out there, you can explore more, slowly building up an image of what's happening in the solar system and what could be causing it to be a part of the loop. It's a mystery that's best explored without any outside help or hints, as that would ruin the purpose. You'll fully immerse yourself in the role of an adventurer and space explorer only by going in blind, noting down what you observe in each cycle, and then starting from scratch once again. Diving headfirst into the unknown, that's where Outer Wilds really shines.

Minecraft

An Infinite World, With Infinite Resources, Ripe For Exploring

There's simply no exploration like the one you experience in Minecraft . Mojang's sandbox survival game puts you inside a world without a quest, without any resources, and with no handholding. You're on your own, and what you make of the world is entirely up to you. Unlike some of the other games here, Minecraft's worlds are procedurally generated, but that doesn't make them any less engaging to explore. There are dozens of biomes and variants, plants and mobs, ores and other resources to find in its Overworld. Throughout the years, a ton of structures from simple dungeons to trial chambers have been added to the mix to make cave diving and adventuring even more rewarding.

That element of randomness, of never knowing what you'll find in the next cave or behind the next hill, is what makes Minecraft so impossibly addictive. And, if you do get bored with the Overworld, you have two other dimensions to explore: the dangerous Nether and the surreal End. While the latter needs a bit more flesh on its bones, still, the Nether has its own set of biomes and a couple of structures to really scratch that insatiable itch for challenge and exploration. In survival, you also have to take into account the cost of exploring by managing your hunger and your health if you get hurt, but once you unlock the Elytra, the world really opens up to you, since you can just fly around with fireworks and cross hundreds of blocks in no time.
